- **Orchestration → `Extraction Router`** (propose), **→ `Status Manager`** (update_status), **→ `Lookup APIs`** (reads), **→ `Change Log`** (changes_since).
- **Blast Radius → `Status Manager`** (Hanging / Active / Deferred / Deprecated transitions), **→ `Extraction Router`** (propose for Adapt), **→ `Atom Store`** (delete for Remove).
- **Hierarchical Nav / Projection / Synthesis / Quality → `Change Log`** for delta subscription, **→ `Lookup APIs`** for reads.
- **Quality's Planted-Contradiction Runner → `Extraction Router`** (inject test fragments).

Reintroduced. The model now distinguishes two groups:

**Present states** (atom remains in the snapshot):
- `Active`, `Hanging`, `Deferred`, `Deprecated`

**Removal outcomes** (atom is taken out of the snapshot; intent recorded in Change Log meta):
- `Superseded` (meta: `superseded_by`)
- `Duplicate` (meta: `duplicate_of`)
- `Rejected` (meta: rationale — for Contradicts / Unclear that didn't survive review)
- `Removed` (meta: rationale — generic, no specific intent)

`update_status` now accepts any of these eight outcomes plus a `meta` argument. Status Manager remains the only mutator.

**Implementation choice:** removal outcomes can be applied as actual deletion (Change Log records the intent + meta; snapshot diff shows the atom gone) OR as a tombstone status on the atom that persists. Both are conformant — the conceptual model treats the outcome as intent regardless of persistence.

- `L3.c4` — Status Manager component description; Iterative Refiner edge to Status Manager labeled with the resolution outcomes it sends.

Promoted to a principle:

> **State is private to its owner; access is API-only.** The principle applies at every level — containers, and components inside a container. State (atom store, indexes, caches, reports, sessions) can be read or mutated only through the operations the owner exposes. No external entity reaches around the API to touch internal storage. This is what makes "the only component that mutates X" claims load-bearing — they're enforced by the principle, not restated as exceptions per consumer.

Removed the Blast-Radius-specific call-outs from `L2/07-blast-radius.md`, `L3/07-blast-radius.md`, and `L3/03-atoms.md`. The principle covers them now.

Summary of new state-owning components:

| Container | New Store Component | What it owns |
|---|---|---|
| Hierarchical Navigation | **Tree Store** | Per-axis tree indexes (the trees + synthesized labels) |
| Blast Radius | **Report Store** | Blast reports + iterative refinement history |
| Blast Radius | **Premise-Tag Index** | Premise-tag → atoms reverse index (kept fresh via Delta Consumer) |
| Projection | **Projection Store** | Rendered projection files in the current snapshot |
| Projection | **Render Cache** (renamed from Cache Manager) | Hash → rendered-prose map |
| Projection | **Reverse Index** (renamed from Reverse Index Maintainer) | Claim-to-section reverse index |

All 11 interface files written. Each follows the same shape: version + types + methods (with signature, errors, idempotency, concurrency) + `changes_since` event variants + version notes + implementer notes.

Cross-cutting facts the contracts establish:

- **9 standard registered use-case keys** for LLM Gateway are part of the `aala-v1` published contract — deployers must wire each to a model.
- **Snapshot lifecycle** is owned by Orchestration; every other container's writes implicitly target `current_snapshot()`.
- **Idempotency keys** are explicit per method: `fragment.message_id` for accept/propose, `(atom_id, outcome, meta.idempotency_key?)` for status updates, `(blast_id, atom_id)` for blast resolutions.
- **Concurrency model** unified: reads concurrent-safe; writes serialized per-snapshot (or per-key) with `ConcurrentWrite` retry as the escape hatch.
- **Change-stream events** typed per container, all sharing the `ChangeEvent<TVariant>` envelope; consumers paginate via `next_ref`.
- **All write paths route through owner components** (Status Manager for Atoms, Projection Store for Projection, Tree Store for Hierarchical Nav, Report Store for Blast Radius) — the contracts make this explicit via the "Notes for implementers" sections referencing the API-only-access principle.

Queued: `docs/spec/` (consolidated formal spec drawing on L2 + L3 + interfaces — adds wire formats, classification semantics, edge cases, conformance) → `docs/implementations/local-mcp-agent/` (implementation of the spec).
I'm assuming the next part: spec is mostly going to stick to prescribing at the L2 container granularity and not go into the L3 components within it.

Yes — confirmed. The spec binds at the **container + interface** granularity, not L3 components. Specifically:

**Normative (spec binds):**
- The 9 containers exist as conceptual units (non-optional ones always; optional ones conditional on being declared).
- Each container's [interface contract](./docs/interfaces/) — method signatures, errors, idempotency, concurrency, event types.
- Cross-container behavior: snapshot coherence, delta-stream semantics (ordering, replay equivalence), `changes_since(ref)` contract.
- The 9 standard LLM Gateway use-case keys.
- The atom data model + atom lifecycle state machine + removal-outcome semantics.
- Conflict classification semantics (exact rules for `New` / `Compatible` / `Refines` / `Supersedes` / etc.).
- Snapshot lifecycle (working / canonical / discarded).
- API-only access principle.

**Informative (the spec references but doesn't bind):**
- L3 component layouts — they describe one valid internal organization; implementations are free to structure differently.
- Specific tools (LiteLLM, git, etc.) — bound only by what they realize from the container interfaces.
- Wire formats — the conceptual data model is normative; the on-the-wire bytes are implementation-defined.
- The example flows in `L2/11-flows.md` — they describe how a complete deployment behaves; partial deployments (with optional containers absent) still conform as long as their composed behavior follows the contracts.

The test of conformance is "can this impl be substituted for a different one without callers noticing, as long as both declare the same set of wired capabilities?" — that question turns on the interface contracts plus a few cross-container behavioral invariants, not on internal component layout.

The spec's job then is to:
1. State which container interfaces an impl must realize (and which it may).
2. Spell out the behavioral invariants that span containers (snapshot coherence, delta-stream replay equivalence, the API-only access enforcement at the boundary).
3. Lock down semantics that span the interface contracts — atom lifecycle, conflict classification rules, edge cases.
4. Define the conformance test posture.

All 14 spec chapters written. Summary of what's bound:

| # | Chapter | Key bindings |
|---|---|---|
| 00 | Introduction | Normative scope; L3 explicitly informative only |
| 01 | Conventions | RFC 2119 keywords, terminology, notation conventions |
| 02 | Conformance | 4 REQUIRED containers (Ingestion, Atoms, Projection, Orchestration), LLM Gateway REQUIRED unless impl runs zero LLM calls, 4 OPTIONAL containers (Hierarchical Nav, Blast Radius, Synthesis, Quality) |
| 03 | Data Model | Atom shape; 7 atom types (registered taxonomy); 4 present states + 4 removal outcomes; DecisionAtom + DefinitionAtom subtypes |
| 04 | Snapshots | 3 states, allowed transitions, coherence invariant ("reads at one SnapshotId are coherent"), aala-managed vs externally-managed canonicalization |
| 05 | Delta Streams | Total ordering within container, replay equivalence `snapshot(X)+deltas(X→Y)≡snapshot(Y)`, paging, cross-snapshot behavior |
| 06 | Conflict Classification | 8 outcomes with exact semantics; deterministic stage REQUIRED; NN + LLM judge RECOMMENDED; alias resolution rules; decision atom `supersedes` semantics |
| 07 | Atom Lifecycle | Complete transition table (all valid transitions enumerated); Hanging cascade rules (direct refs REQUIRED, premise tags + LLM-implicit RECOMMENDED if Blast wired) |
| 08 | Blast Radius | 3 pipeline stages (direct REQUIRED, premise + implicit RECOMMENDED); status cascade; categorization; iterative refinement MUST terminate; report lifecycle |
| 09 | LLM Gateway | 9 standard use-case keys (REQUIRED); schema validation contract; fallback chain; opt-in caching; observability emission; no content persistence beyond cache + structured records |
| 10 | Error Model | Common variants; retryable vs non-retryable categories; per-method idempotency contracts; atomicity boundaries (per-container, not cross-container) |
| 11 | Versioning | What's patch-compatible vs breaking; version negotiation via `capabilities()`; schema evolution rules per data-model element |
| 12 | Edge Cases | Concurrent proposals, ref-against-discarded-snapshot, snapshot switch mid-op, LLM non-convergence cap, malformed LLM responses, dead refs, circular aliases, empty fragments, idempotency-key disposal |

Restructure design into L2 / L3 folders + add interfaces, spec, and implementation notes

Reorganizes the architecture docs around the capability-as-container principle and
adds the binding artifacts a conformant aala implementation must honor.

Highlights
- L2 collapsed from 16 services into 9 single-concern containers (Ingestion,
  Atoms, Projection, Orchestration, Hierarchical Navigation, Blast Radius,
  Synthesis, LLM Gateway, Quality) following the principle that capabilities
  earn their own container if they hold substantial state, do significant
  derivative work, or are optional/pluggable.
- Atoms absorbs Extraction + Conflict as primitives (single mutation funnel
  via Status Manager; API-only access to state enforced at every level).
- Atom lifecycle revised to four present states (Active / Hanging / Deferred /
  Deprecated) plus four removal outcomes carrying intent (Superseded /
  Duplicate / Rejected / Removed); snapshots provide audit history.
- Ordered delta-stream principle: every stateful container exposes
  changes_since(ref) with replay equivalence; downstream consumers
  self-subscribe.
- L2 split into 14 numbered chapters under docs/L2/; L3 split per-container
  under docs/L3/. Old monolithic L2-containers.md and L3-components.md
  deleted.
- Interface contracts in docs/interfaces/ (one file per container) with
  exact signatures, error variants, idempotency, concurrency semantics, and
  9 standard LLM Gateway use-case keys.
- Formal spec in docs/spec/ (14 chapters) consolidating the binding rules:
  conformance, data model, snapshots, delta streams, conflict classification,
  atom lifecycle, blast radius, LLM gateway, error model, versioning, and
  edge cases.
- LikeC4 sources rewritten for the 9-container model; all 11 diagrams
  regenerated (1 L1 + 1 L2 + 9 L3); orphan PNGs from the prior model
  removed. Export script fixed (the -f filter flag was producing identical
  PNGs across views).
- LLM Gateway scope clarified: aala uses a standard external gateway tool
  (LiteLLM / OpenRouter / Portkey / direct) chosen by the implementer;
  deployer configures which models map to each use-case key.
- Implementation notes for local-mcp-agent captured in docs/implementations/
  local-mcp-agent/NOTES.md as informal design discussion ahead of building.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
